title: Thyroglossal & Branchial Cleft Anomalies: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Thyroglossal & Branchial Cleft Anomalies
Identify key diagnostic features of Thyroglossal & Branchial Cleft Anomalies
Apply clinical reasoning to Thyroglossal & Branchial Cleft Anomalies management
Recognize common pitfalls in Thyroglossal & Branchial Cleft Anomalies

sections.title: Overview of Thyroglossal & Branchial Cleft Anomalies
sections.content:
**Thyroglossal & Branchial Cleft Anomalies**

Thyroglossal duct cyst vs branchial cleft cyst, location and movement characteristics, Sistrunk procedure

**Key Points:**
• Thyroglossal duct cyst: MIDLINE, moves with swallowing AND tongue protrusion, most common congenital midline neck mass in children
• Branchial cleft cyst: LATERAL (anterior to SCM), most are 2nd cleft (angle of mandible)
• Thyroglossal cyst treatment: Sistrunk procedure (excise cyst + central hyoid bone + tract to foramen cecum)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Thyroglossal duct cyst: MIDLINE, moves with swallowing AND tongue protrusion, most common congenital midline neck mass in children
• Branchial cleft cyst: LATERAL (anterior to SCM), most are 2nd cleft (angle of mandible)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Thyroglossal cyst treatment: Sistrunk procedure (excise cyst + central hyoid bone + tract to foramen cecum)
• Must confirm normal thyroid tissue exists before excision (may be only thyroid tissue)
• Infected thyroglossal cyst: antibiotics first, then surgical excision after infection resolves

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Thyroglossal & Branchial Cleft Anomalies:**

• Thyroglossal cyst treatment: Sistrunk procedure (excise cyst + central hyoid bone + tract to foramen cecum)
• Must confirm normal thyroid tissue exists before excision (may be only thyroid tissue)
• Infected thyroglossal cyst: antibiotics first, then surgical excision after infection resolves
